stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant large language model.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its current popularity and performance tracking.
The associated pipeline is text-generation, placing it within the core NLP/LLM application space.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core NLP/ML domain, specifically text generation tasks.
The model is associated with Meta's Llama family, indicating relevance in the large language model space.

stories.title: Photoshop and Premiere now have AI assistants
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Adobe is integrating bespoke AI Assistants across its entire Creative Cloud suite.
New AI features are rolling out to major applications including Photoshop, Premiere, and Illustrator.
This marks a significant industry shift toward embedding generative AI directly into professional creative workflows.

stories.title: Massive breach spills credentials for thousands of sensitive networks
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A massive data breach has exposed credentials across numerous sensitive networks.
High-profile organizations affected include Oracle, Lenovo, FedEx, and Fortinet.
The breach impacts critical infrastructure, including a NATO contractor.

stories.title: AI coding agents taught robots how to install GPUs and cut zip ties
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Nvidia is utilizing AI coding agents to teach robots complex physical tasks.
The AI agents were trained to perform practical actions, such as installing GPUs and cutting zip ties.
This initiative represents a significant step in Nvidia's self-improvement and embodied AI research.

stories.title: "Dangerous" AI models are coming no matter what
stories.author: Lily Hay Newman, WIRED.com
bullet_points: Advanced AI models are predicted to soon possess significant hacking capabilities.
The proliferation of these 'dangerous' AI models is viewed as inevitable.
The story signals a major shift in AI safety and cybersecurity concerns.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and utilizes transformers and safetensors.
It is associated with the deepseek_v3 architecture, indicating relevance in large language model development.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating its generative capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, now integrates MLX for optimized performance.
This update specifically targets and accelerates the experience on Apple Silicon devices.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, signaling ongoing optimization efforts.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Adobe’s redesigned AI studio remembers what your creations look like
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Adobe is rolling out a redesigned AI studio integrated with its Firefly AI assistant.
The new features aim to provide users with 'persistent context' and 'reusable assets' across projects.
The update is currently available in a private beta phase.

stories.title: Tesco moving 40,000 server workloads off VMware amid Broadcom's “abusive conduct”
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Tesco is migrating 40,000 server workloads away from VMware.
The move is reportedly due to Broadcom significantly hiking VMware prices (claimed to be 175% in UK court filings).
This signals a major industry shift in enterprise software vendor lock-in and pricing strategies.

stories.title: Data Intelligence Agents: Interpreting, Modeling, and Querying Enterprise Data via Autonomous Coding Agents
stories.author: Henrik Ohlsson
bullet_points: Introduces Data Intelligence Agents (DIA) to automate enterprise data integration, addressing current bottlenecks.
DIA utilizes a system of three specialized agents (Interpreter, Schema Creator, Query Generator) powered by autonomous coding agents (ACAs).
The core innovation is treating ACAs as a first-class abstraction, enabling agents to generate, execute, validate, and repair code directly.

stories.title: Diffusion-Proof: Recipe for Formal Theorem Proving Beyond Auto-Regressive Generation
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: Addresses the limitations of Auto-Regressive (AR) LLMs in formal theorem proving, specifically concerning long-range coherence and error compounding.
Proposes a new methodology, 'Diffusion-Proof,' to enhance the formal mathematical reasoning capabilities of LLMs.
Highlights the ongoing critical research focus in AI/CS on improving LLMs for rigorous mathematical tasks.

stories.title: I found 10k GitHub repositories distributing Trojan malware
stories.author: theorchid
bullet_points: Discovery of a large-scale malware distribution campaign.
The malware, identified as a Trojan, was found across thousands of GitHub repositories.
Highlights significant security vulnerabilities within open-source code ecosystems.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes 'local-first' operation, allowing AI to run entirely on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, meaning processing occurs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: Who decides when AI is too dangerous?
stories.author: Nilay Patel
bullet_points: The article discusses the complex issue of determining when AI poses a danger.
The discussion features Hayden Field, a senior AI reporter for The Verge.
A recent incident involving Anthropic, the Trump administration, and the Fable 5 model is highlighted as a case study.

stories.title: Midjourney goes from generating cat images to full-body ultrasound scans
stories.author: Richard Lawler
bullet_points: Midjourney is expanding beyond its AI image generation capabilities into hardware.
The new product, 'The Midjourney Scanner,' is an ultrasound-based full-body scanner.
The CEO plans to integrate this technology into a new spa concept in San Francisco.

stories.title: Sooner than expected? Useful quantum error correction promised for 2028.
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Quantum error correction, a critical component for stable quantum computing, is projected to be useful by 2028.
The news highlights the ongoing interplay between advanced quantum hardware and classical computing capabilities.
The topic falls into the niche but highly significant area of quantum information science.

stories.title: California says AT&T lied to FCC in attempt to shut off old phone network
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: California is challenging AT&T's actions regarding the discontinuation of an older phone network.
The core issue involves AT&T's petitions to the FCC to override state regulations.
The FCC is currently reviewing these petitions related to the service shutdown.

stories.title: UBP2: Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning for Efficient Preference-based Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Nicholas Rhinehart
bullet_points: Introduces UBP2, a model-based approach to Preference-based Reinforcement Learning (RL).
Addresses the sample inefficiency of existing methods by actively directing exploration.
Jointly reasons over uncertainties in the reward, dynamics, and value functions.

stories.title: Rethinking Reward Supervision: Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation
stories.author: Rex Ying
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in post-training reasoning language models, specifically concerning expensive and potentially noisy Chain-of-Thought (CoT) annotations used in supervised distillation.
It proposes a novel approach called 'Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation' to improve reward supervision.
The research aims to overcome the shortcomings of traditional Reinforcement Learning (RL) methods that compress complex evaluative feedback into simplified rewards.

stories.title: Reference-Driven Multi-Speaker Audio Scene Generation from In-the-Wild Priors
stories.author: Yoav HaCohen
bullet_points: Introduces ScenA, a new method for multi-speaker audio scene generation.
Differs from traditional systems by moving beyond speech-only pipelines to incorporate ambient, in-the-wild audio texture.
Utilizes a text-to-audio flow-matching foundation model conditioned on multiple reference voices.

stories.title: Explaining Attention with Program Synthesis
stories.author: Jacob Andreas
bullet_points: The research addresses the goal of making deep learning interpretable by replacing opaque neural computations with symbolic descriptions.
The proposed method approximates the behavior of transformer attention heads using executable programs.
The process involves computing attention matrices on training examples and then using a pre-trained language model for synthesis.

stories.title: NeSyCat Torch: A Differentiable Tensor Implementation of Categorical Semantics for Neurosymbolic Learning
stories.author: Björn Gehrke
bullet_points: Introduces NeSyCat Torch, a differentiable tensor implementation designed to unify fragmented semantic definitions (classical, fuzzy, probabilistic, neural) into a single framework.
Addresses a key limitation in the original NeSyCat by integrating the interpretation of computational symbols learned by neural networks.
Contributes to the field of neurosymbolic learning by providing a mechanism to bridge symbolic reasoning with neural network capabilities.

stories.title: A Multi-Domain Benchmark for Detecting AI-Generated Text-Rich Images from GPT-Image-2
stories.author: Yuqi Ouyang
bullet_points: The research addresses the growing challenge of detecting AI-generated text-rich images from advanced models like GPT-Image-2.
Text-rich images pose a significant digital trust risk because they can contain sensitive, transactional, or decision-relevant information.
The study introduces a new multi-domain benchmark to improve detection capabilities beyond existing object-centric benchmarks.

stories.title: X+Slides: Benchmarking Audience-Conditioned Slide Generation
stories.author: Fan Wu
bullet_points: Introduces X+Slides, a new benchmark for evaluating LLM-based slide generation.
Addresses a gap in current benchmarks by incorporating 'audience-conditioning' as a critical factor.
Highlights the need for models to tailor output (e.g., proofs for specialists vs. conclusions for decision-makers).

stories.title: Freeing the Law with LOCUS: A Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States
stories.author: Diag Davenport
bullet_points: LOCUS is a new machine-readable corpus designed to aggregate fragmented local ordinances across the United States.
The project addresses a critical gap in legal AI by providing access to local codes (zoning, housing, licensing, etc.), which are currently difficult to access at scale.
This work is relevant to the growing field of legal AI, enabling better large-scale research and automation in regulatory compliance.

stories.title: UBP2: Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning for Efficient Preference-based Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Nicholas Rhinehart
bullet_points: Introduces UBP2, a model-based approach for Preference-based Reinforcement Learning (RL).
Addresses the sample inefficiency of existing preference-based RL methods by actively directing exploration.
Jointly reasons over uncertainties in the reward, dynamics, and value functions to improve learning efficiency.

stories.title: Explaining Attention with Program Synthesis
stories.author: Jacob Andreas
bullet_points: The research addresses the need for interpretable deep learning by aiming to replace opaque neural computations with symbolic descriptions.
The paper proposes approximating the behavior of deep network components, specifically attention heads in transformer models, using executable programs.
The methodology involves computing attention matrices on training examples and then using a pre-trained language model for program synthesis.

stories.title: Optimal scenario design for climate emulation
stories.author: Noelle E. Selin
bullet_points: The paper addresses the generalization limitations of deep learning surrogate climate models (emulators).
It argues that the low structural diversity in current training datasets is a primary bottleneck for predictive skill.
The research proposes optimizing the training datasets themselves as a method to improve model generalization.

stories.title: Confidence is Not Reliability: Rethinking MC Dropout in Brain Tumour Segmentation
stories.author: Nishant Ravikumar
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical patient safety issue in brain tumor segmentation (glioma) where standard metrics fail to detect errors in vital regions.
It investigates the reliability of using Monte Carlo (MC) Dropout for voxel-level uncertainty estimation in clinical segmentation models.
The study aims to determine if calibration failures in these models can be detected using standard reporting metrics.

stories.title: Does VLA Even Know the Basics? Measuring Commonsense and World Knowledge Retention in Vision-Language-Action Models
stories.author: Vlad Shakhuro
bullet_points: The paper addresses a key gap in Embodied Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models: quantifying retained commonsense and world knowledge after fine-tuning on robotics data.
It proposes 'Act2Answer,' a new protocol that adapts existing VLM knowledge benchmarks to VLA evaluation by forcing agents to answer via action.
The research aims to disentangle failures in VLA models, distinguishing between a lack of underlying knowledge and poor low-level control generalization.

stories.title: Risk Stratification for ICU Delirium using Pervasive Ambient Sensing Information
stories.author: Parisa Rashidi
bullet_points: The research addresses ICU delirium, a serious complication linked to increased patient morbidity and costs.
It investigates the potential of pervasive ambient sensing data (light intensity and sound pressure levels) for early risk stratification.
The study aims to determine if environmental factors can independently predict the onset of delirium, an area often overlooked in current risk assessments.

stories.title: Native Active Perception as Reasoning for Omni-Modal Understanding
stories.author: Pheng-Ann Heng
bullet_points: Introduces OmniAgent, a novel agent designed for native omni-modal video understanding.
Addresses the computational inefficiency of traditional 'watch-it-all' passive video models.
Frames video understanding using a POMDP-based iterative Observation-Thought-Action cycle to manage context cost.

stories.title: Learning User Simulators with Turing Rewards
stories.author: Yoon Kim
bullet_points: Introduces Turing-RL, a new reinforcement learning approach for training user simulators.
Addresses a limitation in existing methods that rely on matching a single ground truth response (e.g., log probability or similarity reward).
The technology has broad applicability across agent assistant training, personalization system evaluation, and social science research.

stories.title: Freeing the Law with LOCUS: A Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States
stories.author: Diag Davenport
bullet_points: LOCUS is a new, machine-readable corpus designed to aggregate local ordinances across the United States.
The project addresses a critical gap in legal AI by providing access to local codes (zoning, housing, licensing, etc.), which are currently fragmented.
This work is significant for advancing legal AI applications that require large-scale, authoritative text data beyond federal or state laws.

stories.title: Rethinking Reward Supervision: Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation
stories.author: Rex Ying
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in post-training reasoning language models, specifically issues with expensive and noisy Chain-of-Thought (CoT) annotations used in supervised distillation.
It critiques Reinforcement Learning (RL) methods by noting that evaluative feedback is often compressed into a single signal.
The proposed method, 'Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation,' aims to improve learning by rethinking how reward supervision is applied.

stories.title: Enhancing Decision-Making with Large Language Models through Multi-Agent Fictitious Play
stories.author: Tat-Seng Chua
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in current LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) when handling complex decision-making tasks.
Traditional 'divide-and-conquer' approaches fail when decisions require simultaneous, mutually dependent reasoning from multiple stakeholders.
The proposed method utilizes 'Multi-Agent Fictitious Play' to enhance LLM decision-making capabilities in such scenarios.

stories.title: Structured Inference with Large Language Gibbs
stories.author: Esmeralda S. Whitammer
bullet_points: Introduces 'Large Language Gibbs,' a new method for structured probabilistic inference.
Addresses the challenge of accessing LLM knowledge in a probabilistically coherent manner for complex reasoning.
Uses the conditional distributions of an LLM as transition operators, moving beyond single-pass autoregressive generation.

stories.title: I Audited 3 AI Coding Tools for Privacy — The Difference Is 100x
stories.author: yurenpai_c188178e6b313e59
bullet_points: The article compares the privacy practices of three AI coding tools: Claude Code, Cursor, and Cline.
Key privacy concerns highlighted include Claude Code storing large conversation logs (43MB) and Cline archiving all user input.
Cursor is noted for saving passwords in JSONL format, suggesting a specific data handling method.
